seoTitle: SmartSens Authorized Distributor | CMOS Image Sensors | BeiLuo Electronics
seoDescription: Authorized distributor of SmartSens CMOS image sensors for security, automotive, industrial, and consumer applications. Comprehensive product selection and technical support.

faqs.question: What are the main product categories offered by SmartSens?
faqs.answer: SmartSens offers 4 core product categories: Security Surveillance Sensors, Automotive Image Sensors, Industrial Machine Vision Sensors, and Consumer Electronics Sensors. Each category offers multiple series optimized for specific applications with advanced features like low-light performance, HDR, and AI-ready capabilities.
faqs.decisionGuide: Click on any product category to explore detailed specifications, available models, and application guidance.

faqs.question: Is BeiLuo Electronics an authorized SmartSens distributor?
faqs.answer: Yes, BeiLuo Electronics is an authorized distributor of SmartSens products. We maintain direct partnerships with SmartSens Technology to ensure our customers receive genuine products with full manufacturer warranty and technical support. As an authorized distributor, we have access to SmartSens' complete product portfolio, including their latest CMOS image sensors for security, automotive, industrial, and consumer applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: When purchasing SmartSens products, always verify that your supplier is an authorized distributor to ensure product authenticity and warranty coverage. Contact us for a copy of our authorization certificate.

faqs.question: What are SmartSens' core competitive advantages?
faqs.answer: SmartSens has established several key competitive advantages including proprietary pixel technologies like LightGuiding pixel, SFCPixel, and PixGain HDR that deliver exceptional image quality. Their comprehensive product portfolio covers security surveillance, automotive, industrial, and consumer markets. Strong focus on low-light performance makes their sensors ideal for 24/7 surveillance. AI-ready sensors support intelligent analytics, and automotive-grade products meet stringent AEC-Q100 standards.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider SmartSens for projects requiring excellent low-light performance, WDR capabilities, or AI-ready imaging solutions. Their specialized focus on security and automotive markets provides significant advantages.

faqs.question: How do I select the right SmartSens image sensor?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right SmartSens image sensor involves evaluating resolution requirements, optical format for lens matching, low-light performance needs, required features like HDR and LED flicker mitigation, and interface requirements. For automotive applications, ensure AEC-Q100 qualification. Contact our FAE team for detailed selection guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our product selection guide or contact our FAE team with your specific requirements. We can provide comparison matrices, sample evaluation, and recommendations.

categories.name: Security Surveillance Sensors
categories.description: High-performance CMOS image sensors optimized for security cameras, featuring excellent low-light sensitivity, WDR, and AI-ready capabilities for professional surveillance applications.
categories.longDescription: SmartSens Security Surveillance Sensors are specifically designed for professional security and surveillance applications. As an authorized SmartSens distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides comprehensive product selection guidance for these high-performance sensors. The SC Series features proprietary pixel technologies including LightGuiding pixel and SFCPixel that deliver exceptional low-light performance down to 0.001 lux. With advanced WDR up to 120dB and AI-ready output formats, these sensors are ideal for IP cameras, analog cameras, and smart surveillance systems requiring 24/7 operation in challenging lighting conditions.
categories.selectionGuide: Comprehensive guide to choosing the optimal SmartSens security sensor based on your surveillance requirements

faqs.question: What makes SmartSens security sensors ideal for low-light surveillance?
faqs.answer: SmartSens security sensors feature proprietary pixel technologies like LightGuiding pixel and SFCPixel that significantly improve light gathering efficiency. The SC Series achieves minimum illumination down to 0.001 lux in color mode, enabling clear imaging in near-total darkness. Advanced noise reduction algorithms and high sensitivity pixels ensure excellent image quality even in challenging nighttime conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: For 24/7 surveillance applications requiring excellent night vision, SmartSens SC Series sensors provide industry-leading low-light performance without requiring additional IR illumination in many scenarios.

faqs.question: What WDR capabilities do SmartSens security sensors offer?
faqs.answer: SmartSens security sensors offer advanced WDR (Wide Dynamic Range) capabilities up to 120dB using PixGain HDR technology. This enables clear imaging in high-contrast scenarios such as entrances with bright outdoor light and darker indoor areas. The sensors can capture detail in both bright and dark areas simultaneously, essential for professional surveillance applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable WDR mode for cameras monitoring entrances, windows, or areas with mixed lighting conditions. The 120dB WDR handles challenging backlight scenarios effectively.

faqs.question: Are SmartSens security sensors AI-ready for intelligent analytics?
faqs.answer: Yes, SmartSens AI Series security sensors are specifically designed for intelligent analytics applications. They feature AI-ready output formats that reduce bandwidth and processing requirements while maintaining high detection accuracy. These sensors support facial recognition, object detection, and behavior analysis when paired with edge AI processors.
faqs.decisionGuide: For AI-powered surveillance systems, choose AI Series sensors and pair them with compatible edge AI processors for real-time intelligent analytics.

faqs.question: What resolutions are available for SmartSens security sensors?
faqs.answer: SmartSens offers security sensors ranging from 2MP to 8MP resolutions. The SC2336 provides 2MP resolution suitable for general surveillance, while the SC830AI offers 8MP resolution for applications requiring fine detail such as facial recognition and license plate reading. Higher resolution sensors are available for specialized applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose 2MP sensors for general surveillance and cost-sensitive applications. Select 4MP or 8MP sensors for applications requiring fine detail recognition or large area coverage.

faqs.question: Do SmartSens security sensors support LED flicker mitigation?
faqs.answer: Yes, SmartSens security sensors include LED flicker mitigation (LFM) technology that prevents image flickering when capturing LED light sources. This is essential for accurate color reproduction and reliable imaging in environments with LED lighting, street lights, and vehicle headlights.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable LED flicker mitigation for outdoor surveillance cameras and applications where LED lighting is prevalent to ensure accurate color reproduction.

faqs.question: What interface options are available for SmartSens security sensors?
faqs.answer: SmartSens security sensors support MIPI CSI-2 interface with multiple lanes for high-speed data transmission. They also support DVP parallel interface for compatibility with legacy systems. The MIPI interface supports up to 4 lanes, providing sufficient bandwidth for high-resolution sensors at high frame rates.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use MIPI CSI-2 for new designs to benefit from higher bandwidth and reliability. DVP is available for legacy system compatibility.

products.name: 2MP Ultra-Low Light Security Sensor
products.shortDescription: High-performance 2MP security sensor with exceptional low-light sensitivity and 120dB WDR for professional surveillance.
descriptionParagraphs: The SC2336 is a 2-megapixel CMOS image sensor specifically designed for professional security and surveillance applications. Featuring SmartSens' proprietary LightGuiding pixel technology, this sensor achieves exceptional low-light performance with minimum illumination down to 0.001 lux in color mode. The 1/2.8 inch optical format with 2.9μm pixels delivers excellent sensitivity and low noise performance.

This sensor incorporates advanced PixGain HDR technology providing up to 120dB dynamic range for clear imaging in high-contrast lighting conditions. The SC2336 supports multiple output formats including 12-bit RAW and compressed formats optimized for video streaming. With support for up to 60fps at 1080p resolution, it delivers smooth video for real-time surveillance applications.

The SC2336 includes LED flicker mitigation technology for accurate imaging under LED lighting, making it ideal for both indoor and outdoor surveillance. Its compact package and low power consumption make it suitable for IP cameras, dome cameras, and bullet cameras requiring 24/7 operation.

As an authorized SmartSens distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides comprehensive technical support for the SC2336 including application guidance, schematic review, and image quality tuning assistance.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.content: The SC2336 has become our go-to sensor for professional security camera applications. The low-light performance is truly exceptional - we've achieved usable color images in conditions where other sensors fail completely. The 120dB WDR handles entrance monitoring scenarios very well, capturing both indoor and outdoor details simultaneously. During our extensive testing, the LED flicker mitigation worked effectively with modern LED street lighting. Power consumption is as specified, making it suitable for PoE camera designs. The MIPI interface integrates seamlessly with major SoC platforms including HiSilicon and Ambarella.

alternativeParts.reason: Lower cost alternative with similar specs
alternativeParts.comparison: Similar 2MP resolution with slightly reduced low-light performance

faqs.question: What is the minimum illumination for SC2336?
faqs.answer: The SC2336 achieves minimum illumination of 0.001 lux in color mode and 0.0001 lux in monochrome mode. This exceptional low-light performance enables clear imaging in near-total darkness without requiring IR illumination in many scenarios.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extremely low-light applications, the SC2336 provides excellent performance. Consider supplemental IR illumination only for complete darkness scenarios.

faqs.question: Does SC2336 support day/night switching?
faqs.answer: Yes, the SC2336 supports automatic day/night switching with IR cut filter control. The sensor can detect ambient light levels and automatically switch between color mode for daytime and monochrome mode for nighttime operation, optimizing image quality for varying lighting conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable day/night switching for outdoor cameras and applications requiring 24/7 operation. The automatic switching ensures optimal image quality around the clock.

faqs.question: What lens options are compatible with SC2336?
faqs.answer: The SC2336 with 1/2.8 inch optical format is compatible with standard CS-mount and M12-mount lenses. Lens focal length selection depends on the desired field of view - typical choices range from 2.8mm for wide-angle coverage to 12mm for narrow field of view applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select lens focal length based on monitoring distance and coverage area requirements. Use 2.8-4mm for general surveillance and 6-12mm for long-distance monitoring.

products.name: 8MP AI-Ready Security Sensor
products.shortDescription: Advanced 8MP security sensor with AI acceleration support and enhanced analytics for intelligent surveillance systems.
descriptionParagraphs: The SC830AI is a high-performance 8-megapixel CMOS image sensor designed for AI-powered security and surveillance applications. With its 1/2.7 inch optical format and advanced 2.0μm pixels, it delivers superior image quality for facial recognition, license plate reading, and object detection applications requiring high resolution.

This sensor features AI-ready output formats optimized for edge AI processors, reducing bandwidth requirements while maintaining detection accuracy. The enhanced PixGain HDR provides 120dB dynamic range for reliable operation in challenging lighting conditions. Advanced noise reduction ensures clean images even in low-light scenarios with minimum illumination of 0.003 lux.

The SC830AI includes integrated motion detection with programmable zones for event-triggered recording. Its high frame rate capability of 30fps at 8MP resolution supports real-time analytics applications. The sensor's low power design makes it suitable for always-on AI camera systems requiring continuous operation.

As an authorized SmartSens distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides comprehensive technical support for integrating the SC830AI with major AI platforms including Ambarella, HiSilicon, and Rockchip solutions.

faeReview.author: David Wang
faeReview.content: The SC830AI represents a significant advancement in AI-ready security imaging. The 8MP resolution provides the detail needed for accurate facial recognition and license plate reading at reasonable distances. During our evaluation with various AI processors, the AI-ready output formats significantly reduced processing load while maintaining excellent detection accuracy. The 120dB HDR handles challenging lighting scenarios effectively, and the low-light performance exceeds specifications. Integration with Ambarella CV25 and HiSilicon 3516 platforms was straightforward. This sensor has been successfully deployed in multiple intelligent surveillance projects with excellent results.

companionParts.relationship: Industrial sensor for hybrid security systems
Automotive AI sensor for smart parking

faqs.question: Which AI processors are compatible with SC830AI?
faqs.answer: The SC830AI is compatible with major edge AI processors including Ambarella CV2/CV25 series, HiSilicon 3516/3519 series, Rockchip RV1109/RV1126, and Novatek NT9856x. The MIPI CSI-2 interface and AI-ready output formats work with most modern AI SoCs designed for camera applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify compatibility with your chosen AI processor. The sensor works best with processors supporting MIPI CSI-2 and hardware AI acceleration.

faqs.question: What is the maximum cable length for SC830AI?
faqs.answer: With MIPI CSI-2 interface, the SC830AI supports cable lengths up to 30cm with standard flex cables. For longer distances, use MIPI signal conditioning chips or consider using an ISP with longer reach capabilities.
faqs.decisionGuide: For long cable runs in large camera systems, implement signal conditioning or use intermediate ISP chips to extend reach.

faqs.question: Does SC830AI support H.265 encoding?
faqs.answer: The SC830AI outputs RAW image data that can be encoded by the connected SoC. H.265 encoding support depends on the specific processor used with the sensor. Most modern AI SoCs compatible with the SC830AI support H.265 hardware encoding.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify H.265 encoding capability in your chosen SoC. The SC830AI provides high-quality input for efficient H.265 compression.

products.name: 5MP AI-Powered Security Sensor
products.shortDescription: 5MP security sensor with AI acceleration and enhanced low-light performance for intelligent surveillance.
descriptionParagraphs: The SC500AI is a 5-megapixel CMOS image sensor designed for AI-powered security applications requiring a balance of resolution and performance. With its 1/2.8 inch optical format and 2.0μm pixels, it delivers excellent image quality for facial recognition and behavior analysis.

This sensor features AI-ready output formats optimized for edge AI processors, reducing bandwidth requirements while maintaining detection accuracy. The enhanced PixGain HDR provides 100dB dynamic range for reliable operation in challenging lighting conditions.

The SC500AI achieves minimum illumination of 0.005 lux in color mode, enabling clear imaging in low-light scenarios. Its high frame rate capability of 60fps at 5MP resolution supports real-time analytics applications.

faeReview.author: David Wang
faeReview.content: The SC500AI hits the sweet spot for many AI surveillance applications. The 5MP resolution provides excellent detail for facial recognition while maintaining good low-light performance. During our testing, the AI-ready output formats reduced processor load by approximately 30% compared to standard RAW output. The 60fps capability is particularly valuable for capturing fast-moving objects. This sensor has been successfully deployed in retail analytics and access control systems with excellent results.

faqs.question: What AI processors are compatible with SC500AI?
faqs.answer: The SC500AI is compatible with major AI processors including Ambarella CV22/CV25, HiSilicon 3516/3519, Rockchip RV1126/RV1109, and Novatek NT98566. The AI-ready output format is supported by most modern AI SoCs designed for surveillance applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify AI processor compatibility in your design. The SC500AI works with most modern surveillance AI SoCs.

faqs.question: Does SC500AI support WDR for challenging lighting?
faqs.answer: Yes, the SC500AI features 100dB PixGain HDR WDR support. This enables clear imaging in high-contrast scenarios such as entrances with bright outdoor light and darker indoor areas, capturing detail in both bright and dark areas simultaneously.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable WDR mode for cameras monitoring entrances, windows, or areas with mixed lighting conditions.

products.name: 2MP AI-Enhanced Security Sensor
products.shortDescription: 2MP security sensor with AI acceleration and ultra-low-light performance for cost-effective intelligent surveillance.
descriptionParagraphs: The SC223A is a 2-megapixel CMOS image sensor designed for cost-effective AI-powered security applications. With its 1/2.8 inch optical format and advanced 2.9μm pixels, it delivers excellent image quality with enhanced low-light sensitivity.

This sensor features AI-ready output formats optimized for edge AI processors, enabling intelligent analytics on cost-sensitive camera designs. The sensor achieves minimum illumination of 0.002 lux in color mode.
The SC223A supports 60fps at 2MP resolution for smooth video capture and real-time analytics. Its low power consumption makes it ideal for battery-powered and solar-powered camera systems.

faeReview.author: Jennifer Liu
faeReview.content: The SC223A is an excellent choice for cost-sensitive AI camera applications. The AI acceleration feature brings intelligent analytics capabilities to entry-level camera designs. During our evaluation, the low-light performance exceeded expectations for a sensor in this price range. The 60fps capability is a standout feature typically found in higher-end sensors. This sensor is perfect for mass-market AI cameras and smart doorbell applications.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption of SC223A?
faqs.answer: The SC223A features low power consumption of approximately 120mW in active mode, making it ideal for battery-powered and solar-powered camera applications. Power consumption can be further reduced using the sensor's standby and low-power modes.
faqs.decisionGuide: For battery-powered applications, the SC223A provides excellent power efficiency while maintaining AI capabilities.

faqs.question: Does SC223A support motion detection?
faqs.answer: Yes, the SC223A includes integrated motion detection with programmable zones for event-triggered recording and AI analytics. This feature reduces system power consumption and bandwidth by only processing video when motion is detected.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable motion detection for battery-powered and bandwidth-constrained applications.

categories.name: Automotive Image Sensors
categories.description: Automotive-grade CMOS sensors for ADAS, surround view, and in-cabin monitoring with AEC-Q100 qualification and advanced safety features.
categories.longDescription: SmartSens Automotive Image Sensors are specifically designed and qualified for automotive applications requiring high reliability and performance. As an authorized SmartSens distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides comprehensive product selection guidance for these AEC-Q100 qualified sensors. The AT Series features advanced technologies including LED flicker mitigation, HDR, and functional safety support for ADAS applications. These sensors meet stringent automotive standards and are ideal for surround view systems, forward-facing cameras, driver monitoring, and autonomous driving applications.
categories.selectionGuide: Automotive image sensor selection guide for ADAS and surround view applications

faqs.question: What AEC-Q100 grades are available for SmartSens automotive sensors?
faqs.answer: SmartSens automotive image sensors are available in AEC-Q100 Grade 2 (-40°C to +105°C) and Grade 3 (-40°C to +85°C) qualifications. Grade 2 sensors are recommended for engine compartment and exterior applications, while Grade 3 sensors are suitable for interior cabin applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose Grade 2 for exterior and harsh environment applications. Grade 3 is sufficient for interior cabin monitoring applications.

faqs.question: Do these sensors support HDR for challenging lighting conditions?
faqs.answer: Yes, all SmartSens automotive sensors support HDR (High Dynamic Range) imaging with up to 120dB dynamic range. This enables clear imaging in high-contrast scenarios such as entering/exiting tunnels, driving toward sunset, and nighttime driving with oncoming headlights.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable HDR mode for all automotive applications to ensure reliable imaging across varying lighting conditions.

faqs.question: What is LED flicker mitigation and why is it important?
faqs.answer: LED flicker mitigation (LFM) is a technology that prevents image flickering when capturing LED traffic lights, taillights, and signs. Without LFM, LED light sources may appear to flicker or be invisible in camera images due to their pulsing nature.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure LED flicker mitigation is enabled for forward-facing cameras that need to detect traffic lights and vehicle lights.

faqs.question: Are these sensors suitable for surround view camera systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, SmartSens automotive sensors are ideal for surround view systems. The 2MP SC200AI provides sufficient resolution for parking assistance, while higher resolution sensors offer enhanced detail for advanced surround view applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SC200AI for basic surround view systems. Choose higher resolution sensors for premium systems requiring higher detail.

faqs.question: What interface options are available for automotive sensors?
faqs.answer: SmartSens automotive sensors support MIPI CSI-2 interface with 1-4 lanes for high-speed data transmission. They also support parallel DVP interface for compatibility with legacy systems. The MIPI interface includes error detection features for reliable data transmission.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use MIPI CSI-2 for new designs to benefit from higher bandwidth and reliability features. DVP is available for legacy system compatibility.

faqs.question: Do these sensors meet functional safety requirements?
faqs.answer: SmartSens automotive sensors are designed with functional safety considerations and support ASIL-B level applications when combined with appropriate system-level safety measures. The sensors include built-in self-test features and diagnostic capabilities.
faqs.decisionGuide: For ASIL-B and higher safety requirements, implement appropriate system-level safety measures and contact our automotive FAE team for guidance.

products.name: 2MP AEC-Q100 Automotive Image Sensor
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q100 Grade 2 qualified 2MP automotive sensor with HDR and LED flicker mitigation for ADAS applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The SC200AI is a 2-megapixel automotive-grade CMOS image sensor qualified to AEC-Q100 Grade 2 standards. It operates reliably across the full automotive temperature range of -40°C to +105°C, making it suitable for exterior camera applications including rear-view cameras, surround view systems, and forward-facing ADAS cameras.

This sensor features advanced HDR technology with up to 120dB dynamic range, enabling clear imaging in high-contrast scenarios. The built-in LED flicker mitigation ensures reliable detection of LED traffic lights and vehicle lights. With 1/2.8 inch optical format and 2.9μm pixels, it delivers excellent low-light performance for nighttime driving applications.

The SC200AI supports MIPI CSI-2 interface with error detection, ensuring reliable data transmission in automotive electrical environments. Its compact package and robust design make it ideal for space-constrained automotive camera modules.

As an authorized SmartSens distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides comprehensive technical support for automotive applications including AEC-Q100 qualification assistance and PPAP documentation support.

faeReview.author: James Liu
faeReview.content: The SC200AI has been extensively tested in automotive environments and consistently meets AEC-Q100 Grade 2 requirements. The HDR performance is impressive, capturing clear images when transitioning between bright sunlight and dark tunnels. LED flicker mitigation works effectively with modern LED traffic lights and vehicle lighting. We've successfully integrated this sensor in multiple Tier 1 automotive projects for rear-view and surround view cameras. The reliability and image quality meet OEM requirements for mass production.

faqs.question: What is the minimum illumination for SC200AI?
faqs.answer: The SC200AI achieves excellent low-light performance with minimum illumination of 0.1 lux in color mode and 0.01 lux in monochrome mode. This enables reliable nighttime driving assistance. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extremely low-light applications, consider using IR illumination to supplement the sensor's natural low-light capabilities.

faqs.question: Is SC200AI suitable for autonomous driving applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, the SC200AI is designed for autonomous driving camera systems. Its AEC-Q100 Grade 2 qualification and ASIL-B support make it suitable for safety-critical autonomous driving perception systems. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: For autonomous driving, implement multiple sensors with redundancy. The SC200AI provides the reliability needed for perception systems.

faqs.question: What is the typical power consumption of SC200AI?
faqs.answer: The SC200AI has typical power consumption of 150mW in active mode, making it suitable for automotive applications with strict power budgets. Power consumption may vary based on frame rate and operating mode.
faqs.decisionGuide: For power-sensitive automotive applications, the SC200AI's efficient power consumption helps meet system power requirements.

products.name: 1.2MP AEC-Q100 Automotive Image Sensor
products.shortDescription: High-sensitivity 1.2MP automotive sensor with AEC-Q100 Grade 2 qualification for advanced surround view applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The SC120AT is a high-sensitivity 1.2-megapixel automotive-grade CMOS image sensor qualified to AEC-Q100 Grade 2 standards. With its 1/2.8 inch optical format and advanced pixel technology, it delivers superior image quality for demanding automotive applications including surround view cameras, parking assistance systems, and blind spot detection.

This sensor features enhanced low-light performance with minimum illumination down to 0.01 lux, enabling reliable operation in parking garages and nighttime conditions. The 120dB HDR capability handles challenging lighting scenarios, while LED flicker mitigation ensures accurate detection of traffic signals and vehicle lights.

Designed for surround view applications, the SC120AT provides the optimal balance of resolution and low-light performance. Its compact form factor and robust design ensure reliable operation in harsh automotive environments. The sensor supports flexible output configurations for various system architectures.
As an authorized SmartSens distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides comprehensive support for surround view system integration including lens selection guidance and image stitching optimization.

faeReview.author: Robert Zhang
faeReview.content: The SC120AT is an excellent choice for surround view camera systems. The 1.2MP resolution provides sufficient detail for parking assistance while the large 3.0μm pixels deliver exceptional low-light performance. We've successfully deployed this sensor in multiple surround view projects with excellent results. The HDR performance handles transitions between bright outdoor light and dark parking garages effectively. The wide field of view compatibility makes it ideal for fisheye lens applications common in surround view systems. Integration with major automotive SoCs is straightforward.
highlight: Excellent low-light performance for parking garages
Wide FOV compatibility for fisheye lenses

faqs.question: What makes SC120AT ideal for surround view applications?
faqs.answer: The SC120AT is optimized for surround view with its 1.2MP resolution providing sufficient detail for parking assistance while maintaining good low-light performance. The sensor supports wide field of view lenses including fisheye lenses commonly used in 360-degree camera systems. Its compact size allows for discreet placement around the vehicle.
faqs.decisionGuide: For surround view systems requiring excellent low-light performance and wide FOV compatibility, the SC120AT is an ideal choice.

faqs.question: Does SC120AT support fisheye lens correction?
faqs.answer: The SC120AT outputs raw image data that can be processed by the connected SoC for fisheye lens correction. Most modern automotive SoCs support hardware-based lens distortion correction for fisheye lenses used in surround view applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify fisheye correction capability in your chosen SoC. The SC120AT provides high-quality input for effective distortion correction and image stitching.

faqs.question: What is the typical latency of SC120AT?
faqs.answer: The SC120AT has minimal processing latency, typically less than one frame time. This low latency is essential for real-time automotive applications like surround view systems where immediate visual feedback is critical for driver safety.
faqs.decisionGuide: For real-time automotive applications, the SC120AT's low latency ensures responsive visual feedback for drivers.

products.name: 1MP AEC-Q100 Automotive Image Sensor
products.shortDescription: Cost-effective 1MP automotive sensor with AEC-Q100 Grade 2 qualification for rear-view and parking assistance cameras.
descriptionParagraphs: The SC100AI is a cost-effective 1-megapixel automotive-grade CMOS image sensor qualified to AEC-Q100 Grade 2 standards. With its 1/4 inch optical format, it delivers reliable image quality for entry-level automotive applications including rear-view cameras and basic parking assistance systems.

This sensor features 120dB HDR capability for handling challenging lighting scenarios, and LED flicker mitigation ensures accurate detection of traffic signals and vehicle lights. The compact form factor and low power consumption make it ideal for space-constrained automotive designs.
The SC100AI supports standard automotive interfaces and is compatible with major automotive SoCs. Its robust design ensures reliable operation across the automotive temperature range.

faeReview.author: James Liu
faeReview.content: The SC100AI provides an excellent cost-effective solution for entry-level automotive camera applications. While the resolution is lower than premium sensors, the image quality is perfectly adequate for rear-view and basic parking assistance. The AEC-Q100 Grade 2 qualification ensures reliability in automotive environments. We've successfully integrated this sensor in budget-friendly rear-view camera systems with excellent results. The low power consumption is particularly beneficial for always-on camera systems.

faqs.question: Is 1MP resolution sufficient for rear-view cameras?
faqs.answer: Yes, 1MP (1280x720) resolution is sufficient for basic rear-view and parking assistance applications. While higher resolutions provide more detail, 1MP offers adequate clarity for obstacle detection and parking guidance at a lower cost point. For surround view systems or advanced ADAS, higher resolution sensors are recommended.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SC100AI for basic rear-view applications. Upgrade to SC200AI for surround view or detailed object recognition.

faqs.question: Does SC100AI support HDR for automotive lighting?
faqs.answer: Yes, the SC100AI features 120dB HDR capability, enabling clear imaging in challenging automotive lighting scenarios such as exiting tunnels or driving at sunrise/sunset. The HDR ensures details are visible in both bright and dark areas of the scene.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable HDR mode for all automotive applications to handle varying lighting conditions.

products.name: 8MP AEC-Q100 Automotive Image Sensor
products.shortDescription: High-resolution 8MP automotive sensor with AEC-Q100 Grade 2 qualification for advanced ADAS and autonomous driving.
descriptionParagraphs: The SC820 is a high-performance 8-megapixel automotive-grade CMOS image sensor qualified to AEC-Q100 Grade 2 standards. With its 1/2.8 inch optical format and advanced 2.0μm pixels, it delivers exceptional image quality for demanding ADAS and autonomous driving applications.

This sensor features enhanced 140dB HDR capability for handling extreme lighting scenarios, and advanced LED flicker mitigation ensures reliable detection of all traffic signals. The high resolution enables accurate object detection and classification at extended distances.

The SC820 supports AI-ready output formats optimized for automotive AI processors. Its robust design and comprehensive safety features make it ideal for forward-facing ADAS cameras and autonomous driving systems.

faeReview.author: Robert Zhang
faeReview.content: The SC820 represents the pinnacle of SmartSens automotive imaging technology. The 8MP resolution provides the detail necessary for advanced ADAS features like pedestrian detection and traffic sign recognition at highway speeds. The 140dB HDR is exceptional - we've tested it in the most challenging lighting conditions including direct sunlight to dark tunnel transitions. The AI-ready output significantly reduces processing load on the ADAS SoC. This sensor is suitable for premium ADAS and autonomous driving applications.

alternativeParts.comparison: 1.2MP with enhanced low-light for surround view

faqs.question: What ADAS features benefit from 8MP resolution?
faqs.answer: 8MP resolution enables: 1) Longer detection distances for early hazard warning; 2) Accurate traffic sign recognition at highway speeds; 3) Precise lane detection for LKA/LDW systems; 4) Reliable pedestrian and cyclist detection; 5) Detailed object classification. The high resolution provides the pixel density needed for AI algorithms to make accurate decisions at the distances required for safe autonomous operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SC820 for premium ADAS and autonomous driving. Use SC200AI for standard ADAS features.

faqs.question: Does SC820 support functional safety requirements?
faqs.answer: The SC820 is designed with automotive functional safety in mind and supports ISO 26262 development processes. The sensor includes comprehensive diagnostic features and fault detection mechanisms required for ASIL-compliant systems. Contact SmartSens for detailed safety documentation and FMEDA analysis for your specific safety requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: For ASIL-compliant systems, work with SmartSens to obtain complete safety documentation and perform system-level safety analysis.

categories.name: Industrial Machine Vision Sensors
categories.description: High-speed, high-resolution sensors for industrial inspection, robotics, and machine vision applications with global shutter technology.
categories.longDescription: SmartSens Industrial Machine Vision Sensors are engineered for demanding industrial automation applications requiring high speed, precision, and reliability. As an authorized SmartSens distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides expert guidance for selecting the optimal machine vision sensors. The GS Series features global shutter technology that eliminates motion blur in high-speed applications, while the CS Series offers high-resolution imaging for detailed inspection tasks. These sensors support various industrial interfaces and are ideal for factory automation, robotics, quality inspection, and measurement systems.
categories.selectionGuide: Industrial machine vision sensor selection guide for automation and inspection applications

faqs.question: What is the difference between global shutter and rolling shutter?
faqs.answer: Global shutter sensors expose all pixels simultaneously, eliminating motion blur in high-speed applications. Rolling shutter sensors expose pixels row by row, which can cause distortion with fast-moving objects. SmartSens GS Series uses global shutter technology ideal for industrial machine vision.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose global shutter sensors for high-speed motion applications. Rolling shutter may be acceptable for static or slow-moving object inspection.

faqs.question: What frame rates are available for industrial sensors?
faqs.answer: SmartSens industrial sensors offer frame rates ranging from 30fps to over 1000fps depending on resolution and model. High-speed models like the SC132GS support up to 120fps at full resolution, while reduced resolution modes can achieve even higher frame rates.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select frame rate based on your application requirements. Higher frame rates are needed for high-speed inspection and motion analysis.

faqs.question: What interfaces are supported for industrial applications?
faqs.answer: SmartSens industrial sensors support MIPI CSI-2, DVP parallel interface, and some models support industrial interfaces through external converters. The MIPI interface provides high bandwidth for high-resolution, high-frame-rate applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use MIPI CSI-2 for new high-performance designs. Consider interface converters for legacy industrial camera systems.

faqs.question: Are these sensors suitable for robotics applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, SmartSens industrial sensors are ideal for robotics applications including visual guidance, object recognition, and quality inspection. The global shutter technology ensures accurate imaging of moving objects, while compact size and low power consumption suit mobile robotics.
faqs.decisionGuide: For robotics applications, consider global shutter sensors for motion applications and ensure compatibility with your robotics processing platform.

faqs.question: What resolutions are available for industrial inspection?
faqs.answer: SmartSens offers industrial sensors ranging from 1MP to 5MP resolutions. Lower resolutions (1-2MP) are suitable for high-speed applications, while higher resolutions (3-5MP) provide detailed imaging for precision inspection tasks.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match resolution to your inspection requirements. Higher resolution for detailed defect detection, lower resolution for high-speed applications.

faqs.question: Do these sensors support industrial temperature ranges?
faqs.answer: Yes, SmartSens industrial sensors are designed for industrial temperature ranges typically -20°C to +70°C or -40°C to +85°C depending on the model. This ensures reliable operation in factory environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify temperature requirements for your industrial environment. Select sensors with appropriate temperature ratings for your application.

products.name: 1.3MP Global Shutter Machine Vision Sensor
products.shortDescription: High-speed 1.3MP global shutter sensor for industrial inspection and robotics applications with 120fps capability.
descriptionParagraphs: The SC132GS is a 1.3-megapixel global shutter CMOS image sensor specifically designed for industrial machine vision applications. Featuring SmartSens' advanced global shutter technology, this sensor eliminates motion blur when capturing fast-moving objects, making it ideal for high-speed inspection and robotics applications.

This sensor supports frame rates up to 120fps at full resolution, enabling real-time inspection of high-speed production lines. The 1/2.0 inch optical format with 4.0μm pixels delivers excellent sensitivity and dynamic range. Advanced features include programmable exposure, external trigger support, and multiple output modes for flexible system integration.

The SC132GS includes industrial-grade reliability features and operates across a wide temperature range of -20°C to +70°C. Its compact package and standard interface options make it compatible with various industrial camera platforms and vision systems.

As an authorized SmartSens distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides comprehensive technical support for machine vision integration including lens selection, lighting recommendations, and system optimization guidance.

faeReview.author: Thomas Anderson
faeReview.content: The SC132GS has proven to be an excellent choice for our high-speed inspection applications. The global shutter technology completely eliminates the motion blur issues we experienced with rolling shutter sensors. At 120fps, we can inspect products moving at high speeds on production lines without any image distortion. The 4.0μm pixels provide good sensitivity even in factory lighting conditions. Integration with our vision processing systems was straightforward using the standard MIPI interface. The external trigger capability allows precise synchronization with production equipment. Overall, a reliable and cost-effective solution for industrial machine vision.
highlight: High 120fps frame rate for fast inspection

alternativeParts.comparison: Similar specs with 60fps max frame rate

faqs.question: What is the advantage of global shutter in SC132GS?
faqs.answer: The global shutter in SC132GS exposes all pixels simultaneously, eliminating motion blur and distortion when capturing fast-moving objects. This is essential for high-speed inspection and robotics applications where objects move quickly through the camera's field of view.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SC132GS global shutter for any application involving moving objects. Rolling shutter sensors may cause image distortion with fast motion.

faqs.question: Does SC132GS support external trigger?
faqs.answer: Yes, the SC132GS supports external trigger input for precise synchronization with production equipment. This allows the sensor to capture images at exact moments in the production cycle, essential for coordinated inspection systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use external trigger mode when precise timing synchronization is required with production equipment or lighting systems.

faqs.question: What lens mount is compatible with SC132GS?
faqs.answer: The SC132GS with 1/2.0 inch optical format is compatible with C-mount and CS-mount industrial lenses. These standard industrial lens mounts provide access to a wide range of high-quality machine vision lenses from various manufacturers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select C-mount or CS-mount lenses based on your working distance and field of view requirements. CS-mount is more compact for space-constrained applications.

products.name: 2MP High-Resolution Machine Vision Sensor
products.shortDescription: High-resolution 2MP rolling shutter sensor for detailed industrial inspection and measurement applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The SC201CS is a 2-megapixel high-resolution CMOS image sensor designed for detailed industrial inspection and measurement applications. With its 1/2.8 inch optical format and 2.9μm pixels, it delivers exceptional image quality for precision inspection tasks requiring fine detail detection.

This sensor supports frame rates up to 60fps at full 2MP resolution, providing smooth imaging for inspection systems. The rolling shutter design offers high sensitivity and low noise performance suitable for static or slow-moving object inspection. Advanced features include region of interest (ROI) readout for higher frame rates on specific areas.

The SC201CS includes programmable gain and exposure controls for adapting to varying lighting conditions in industrial environments. Its compact package and low power consumption make it suitable for compact inspection systems and embedded vision applications.

As an authorized SmartSens distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides comprehensive support for inspection system integration including lighting design recommendations and image processing optimization.

faeReview.author: Kevin Martinez
faeReview.content: The SC201CS delivers excellent image quality for our precision inspection applications. The 2MP resolution provides the detail needed for detecting small defects in electronic components and precision parts. While it's a rolling shutter sensor, the 60fps frame rate is sufficient for our moderate-speed inspection lines. The ROI feature is particularly useful - we can achieve higher frame rates by reading only the regions of interest, improving throughput. The sensor's low noise performance produces clean images even under challenging factory lighting. Integration was straightforward with standard MIPI interface. A cost-effective solution for detailed inspection tasks.

alternativeParts.comparison: Global shutter vs rolling shutter, 1.3MP vs 2MP

companionParts.relationship: Global shutter sensor for motion applications

faqs.question: When should I choose SC201CS over global shutter sensors?
faqs.answer: Choose SC201CS for static or slow-moving object inspection where high resolution is more important than motion capture. Rolling shutter sensors like SC201CS typically offer better sensitivity and lower cost compared to global shutter sensors of similar resolution.
faqs.decisionGuide: For stationary inspection tasks, SC201CS provides excellent value. Choose global shutter only if you need to capture fast-moving objects without blur.

faqs.question: Does SC201CS support ROI readout?
faqs.answer: Yes, the SC201CS supports region of interest (ROI) readout, allowing you to read only specific portions of the image at higher frame rates. This is useful for inspection systems where only certain areas of the image need detailed analysis.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use ROI mode to increase frame rate when inspecting specific regions. This can significantly improve inspection throughput.

faqs.question: What lighting is recommended for SC201CS inspection applications?
faqs.answer: The SC201CS works well with various industrial lighting including LED ring lights, bar lights, and diffused panels. The choice depends on your inspection requirements - bright field lighting for surface defects, dark field for edge detection, and backlighting for dimensional measurement.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select lighting based on the features you need to inspect. Contact our FAE team for lighting design recommendations specific to your application.

products.name: 1.3MP Global Shutter Machine Vision Sensor
products.shortDescription: High-speed 1.3MP global shutter sensor for industrial robotics and high-speed inspection applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The SC130GS is a 1.3-megapixel global shutter CMOS image sensor designed for high-speed industrial applications. With its 1/2.0 inch optical format and 4.0μm pixels, it delivers excellent image quality with exceptional motion capture capabilities.

This sensor supports frame rates up to 210fps at full 1.3MP resolution, making it ideal for high-speed inspection and robotics applications. The global shutter technology eliminates motion blur when capturing fast-moving objects.
The SC130GS features low power consumption and compact package size suitable for embedded vision systems. Its robust design ensures reliable operation in demanding industrial environments.

faeReview.author: Kevin Martinez
faeReview.content: The SC130GS is our go-to sensor for high-speed machine vision applications. The 210fps frame rate captures even the fastest production line movements without blur. The global shutter is essential for our robotics applications - we've eliminated the distortion issues we had with rolling shutter sensors. The larger 4.0μm pixels provide excellent sensitivity, reducing our lighting requirements. This sensor has been rock-solid reliable in our 24/7 production environments.

faqs.question: What is the maximum frame rate of SC130GS?
faqs.answer: The SC130GS supports up to 210fps at full 1.3MP resolution. Higher frame rates up to 500fps are achievable with ROI (Region of Interest) readout at lower resolutions. This makes it ideal for capturing high-speed industrial processes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use full resolution for detailed inspection. Use ROI mode for ultra-high-speed applications where only specific regions need inspection.

products.name: 3MP High-Resolution Machine Vision Sensor
products.shortDescription: High-resolution 3MP rolling shutter sensor for precision inspection and measurement applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The SC301CS is a 3-megapixel high-resolution CMOS image sensor designed for precision industrial inspection and measurement applications. With its 1/2.8 inch optical format and 2.5μm pixels, it delivers exceptional image detail for detecting small defects.

This sensor supports frame rates up to 45fps at full 3MP resolution, providing detailed imaging for precision inspection systems. The rolling shutter design offers high sensitivity and excellent dynamic range.

The SC301CS includes advanced features like ROI readout and programmable controls for adapting to various industrial inspection requirements. Its compact design suits space-constrained inspection systems.

faeReview.author: Kevin Martinez
faeReview.content: The SC301CS provides the resolution we need for detecting microscopic defects in semiconductor and electronic component inspection. The 3MP resolution reveals details that 2MP sensors miss. While the frame rate is lower than some sensors, 45fps is sufficient for our precision inspection applications. The image quality is excellent with low noise and good color accuracy. A great choice for detailed inspection where resolution matters more than speed.

alternativeParts.reason: Lower resolution with higher frame rate

faqs.question: When should I choose SC301CS over SC201CS?
faqs.answer: Choose SC301CS when you need higher resolution for detecting small defects or fine details. The 3MP resolution provides 50% more pixels than 2MP, revealing finer details. Choose SC201CS when frame rate is more important than resolution, as it offers 60fps vs 45fps. For most precision inspection tasks, SC301CS is the better choice.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SC301CS for high-resolution inspection. Use SC201CS when higher frame rate is needed.

categories.name: Consumer Electronics Sensors
categories.description: Compact, power-efficient sensors for smartphones, tablets, and wearable devices with advanced imaging features.
categories.longDescription: SmartSens Consumer Electronics Sensors are designed for mobile and portable devices requiring compact size, low power consumption, and high-quality imaging. As an authorized SmartSens distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides guidance for integrating these sensors into consumer products. These sensors feature advanced pixel technologies, autofocus support, and optimized power management for battery-operated devices. They are ideal for smartphones, tablets, action cameras, drones, and wearable applications.
categories.selectionGuide: Consumer electronics sensor selection guide for mobile and portable devices

faqs.question: What makes these sensors suitable for smartphones?
faqs.answer: SmartSens consumer sensors feature compact CSP packaging, low power consumption, and MIPI CSI-2 interface ideal for smartphone integration. They support autofocus and offer good image quality for mid-range mobile applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For smartphone applications, consider sensor size, power consumption, and interface compatibility with your mobile processor.

faqs.question: What resolutions are available for consumer applications?
faqs.answer: SmartSens offers consumer sensors ranging from 2MP to 13MP resolutions. Lower resolutions are suitable for front-facing cameras and wearables, while higher resolutions target main camera applications in smartphones and tablets.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match resolution to your product positioning. Higher resolution for premium products, lower resolution for cost-sensitive applications.

faqs.question: Do these sensors support autofocus?
faqs.answer: Yes, SmartSens consumer sensors support both contrast-based and PDAF (Phase Detection Auto Focus) autofocus modes. They are compatible with VCM (Voice Coil Motor) actuators commonly used in smartphone cameras.
faqs.decisionGuide: For autofocus applications, pair the sensor with a compatible VCM actuator and ensure your ISP supports the chosen autofocus algorithm.

faqs.question: What is the typical power consumption?
faqs.answer: SmartSens consumer sensors feature ultra-low power consumption, typically 50-150mW depending on resolution and operating mode. Power-saving modes help extend battery life in portable devices. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: For battery-powered devices, select sensors with low power consumption and implement power management features for optimal battery life.

faqs.question: Are these sensors suitable for drone cameras?
faqs.answer: Yes, SmartSens consumer sensors are ideal for drone cameras due to their compact size, light weight, and good image quality. They support the high frame rates needed for FPV (First Person View) applications and stabilization.
faqs.decisionGuide: For drone applications, consider weight, power consumption, and vibration resistance. Global shutter sensors may be preferred for reducing rolling shutter effects.

faqs.question: What interface is used for consumer sensors?
faqs.answer: SmartSens consumer sensors use MIPI CSI-2 interface, the standard for mobile applications. This interface is supported by all major mobile processors and provides high bandwidth for high-resolution video.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure your processor supports MIPI CSI-2 interface. Most modern mobile and consumer processors are compatible with SmartSens sensors.

products.name: 8MP Consumer Image Sensor
products.shortDescription: Compact 8MP sensor for smartphones and tablets with low power consumption and autofocus support.
descriptionParagraphs: The SC800 is an 8-megapixel CMOS image sensor designed for consumer electronics applications including smartphones, tablets, and portable devices. Featuring a compact 1/4 inch optical format, this sensor fits into slim device designs while delivering excellent image quality for mid-range mobile applications.

This sensor incorporates advanced pixel technology with 1.12μm pixels optimized for good low-light performance in consumer applications. It supports both contrast-based and PDAF autofocus modes, enabling fast and accurate focusing for mobile photography. The SC800 supports up to 30fps at full 8MP resolution for smooth video recording.

The SC800 features ultra-low power consumption of approximately 80mW in active mode, helping extend battery life in portable devices. It supports standard MIPI CSI-2 interface for easy integration with major mobile processors from Qualcomm, MediaTek, and other vendors.
As an authorized SmartSens distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides technical support for consumer product integration including camera module design guidance and image tuning assistance.

faeReview.author: Lisa Chen
faeReview.content: The SC800 is an excellent choice for mid-range smartphone applications. The 8MP resolution provides good detail for social media sharing and everyday photography. The compact 1/4 inch format allows for slim camera module designs suitable for modern smartphones. Autofocus performance is reliable with both contrast and PDAF modes. Power consumption is as specified, contributing to good battery life in our test devices. Integration with MediaTek Helio processors was straightforward. Image quality is competitive with other sensors in this price range, making it a good value proposition for cost-sensitive smartphone designs.

companionParts.relationship: Security sensor for camera security features
Industrial sensor for advanced camera features

faqs.question: Is SC800 suitable for front-facing smartphone cameras?
faqs.answer: Yes, the SC800 is an excellent choice for front-facing smartphone cameras. Its 8MP resolution provides good quality for selfies and video calls, while the compact size fits in the limited space available for front cameras. The low power consumption helps preserve battery life during video calls.
faqs.decisionGuide: For front-facing cameras, SC800 offers a good balance of resolution, size, and power consumption. Consider higher resolution sensors for premium selfie cameras.

faqs.question: What processors are compatible with SC800?
faqs.answer: The SC800 is compatible with major mobile processors including Qualcomm Snapdragon series, MediaTek Helio series, Samsung Exynos, and UNISOC platforms. The standard MIPI CSI-2 interface ensures broad compatibility with modern mobile SoCs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify MIPI CSI-2 lane configuration compatibility with your chosen processor. Most modern mobile processors support the 2-lane configuration used by SC800.

faqs.question: Does SC800 support HDR imaging?
faqs.answer: Yes, the SC800 supports HDR (High Dynamic Range) imaging for better detail capture in high-contrast scenes. This is particularly useful for outdoor photography and backlit situations commonly encountered in mobile photography.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable HDR mode for challenging lighting conditions. The HDR feature helps capture more detail in both shadows and highlights.

products.name: 13MP Premium Consumer Image Sensor
products.shortDescription: High-resolution 13MP sensor for premium smartphones with advanced imaging features and 4K video support.
descriptionParagraphs: The SC1300 is a 13-megapixel CMOS image sensor designed for premium consumer electronics applications. With its 1/3 inch optical format and advanced 1.12μm pixels, it delivers exceptional image quality for high-end smartphone cameras and premium portable devices.

This sensor features advanced imaging capabilities including HDR support, enhanced low-light performance, and 4K video recording at 30fps. The SC1300 supports both contrast-based and PDAF autofocus for fast, accurate focusing. Advanced noise reduction algorithms ensure clean images even in challenging lighting conditions.

The SC1300 includes features specifically designed for premium mobile applications including burst mode capture, slow-motion video, and real-time HDR processing. Its MIPI CSI-2 interface supports high-speed data transmission required for high-resolution imaging and 4K video.
As an authorized SmartSens distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides comprehensive support for premium product integration including image quality tuning and camera module optimization.

faeReview.author: Michael Park
faeReview.content: The SC1300 delivers impressive image quality for a 13MP sensor in this price range. The 4K video capability is smooth and detailed, suitable for premium smartphone applications. HDR performance is good, capturing decent dynamic range in high-contrast scenes. Autofocus speed with PDAF is fast and reliable. We've successfully integrated this sensor with Snapdragon 7-series processors with good results. The 4-lane MIPI interface provides sufficient bandwidth for high-resolution capture without bottlenecks. For premium mid-range smartphones, the SC1300 offers a compelling combination of resolution, features, and value.

alternativeParts.reason: Security sensor alternative with low-light focus

companionParts.relationship: Automotive sensor for connected car features

faqs.question: Does SC1300 support 4K video recording?
faqs.answer: Yes, the SC1300 supports 4K video recording at 30fps and 1080p at 60fps. The 4-lane MIPI CSI-2 interface provides sufficient bandwidth for high-resolution video capture without compression artifacts. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: For 4K video applications, ensure your processor supports 4K video processing and has adequate storage and encoding capabilities.

faqs.question: What is the maximum burst capture rate of SC1300?
faqs.answer: The SC1300 supports burst capture mode for capturing multiple frames in rapid succession. The exact burst rate depends on the processor and storage capabilities of the device, but the sensor can output frames at up to 30fps at full 13MP resolution.
faqs.decisionGuide: For burst photography applications, ensure your system has adequate processing power and storage bandwidth to handle high-speed image capture.

faqs.question: Is SC1300 suitable for action camera applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, the SC1300 is suitable for action camera applications with its 13MP resolution and 4K video capability. However, for fast-action sports, consider pairing with electronic image stabilization (EIS) to compensate for motion, as the rolling shutter may produce some distortion with very fast movements.
faqs.decisionGuide: For action cameras, implement EIS for smooth video. The high resolution provides good detail for action photography and video.

products.name: 5MP Consumer Image Sensor
products.shortDescription: 5MP sensor for mid-range smartphones and tablets with balanced performance and power efficiency.
descriptionParagraphs: The SC500 is a 5-megapixel CMOS image sensor designed for mid-range consumer electronics applications. With its 1/4 inch optical format and 1.75μm pixels, it delivers good image quality for mainstream smartphone and tablet cameras.

This sensor supports 1080p video recording at 30fps and includes HDR support for improved dynamic range. The SC500 features a compact design and low power consumption suitable for battery-operated devices.

The SC500 is compatible with major mobile processors and supports standard MIPI CSI-2 interface. Its cost-effective design makes it ideal for mid-range consumer products requiring reliable imaging performance.

faeReview.author: Michael Park
faeReview.content: The SC500 is a solid choice for mid-range smartphone applications where cost and power consumption are important considerations. The 5MP resolution is adequate for most consumer photography needs, and the image quality is good for this price range. Power consumption is reasonable at 90mW, helping extend battery life. We've had success integrating this sensor with MediaTek and UNISOC platforms for budget smartphone designs.

faqs.question: Is 5MP sufficient for smartphone cameras?
faqs.answer: Yes, 5MP resolution is sufficient for most smartphone photography needs including social media sharing, video calls, and casual photography. While higher resolutions offer more detail, 5MP provides good image quality for typical consumer use cases and helps keep costs down for budget devices.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SC500 for budget and mid-range devices. Upgrade to SC800 or SC1300 for premium devices requiring higher resolution.

products.name: 2MP Compact Consumer Image Sensor
products.shortDescription: Compact 2MP sensor for wearable devices and IoT applications with ultra-low power consumption.
descriptionParagraphs: The SC200 is a 2-megapixel CMOS image sensor designed for compact consumer and IoT applications. With its small 1/5 inch optical format and 1.75μm pixels, it delivers adequate image quality for space-constrained devices.

This sensor features ultra-low power consumption of only 60mW, making it ideal for battery-powered wearable devices and IoT cameras. The compact package size enables integration into small form factor products.

The SC200 supports 720p video recording and includes basic imaging features suitable for simple photography and video applications. Its low cost and small size make it perfect for cost-sensitive IoT and wearable products.

faeReview.author: Jennifer Liu
faeReview.content: The SC200 is perfect for wearable and IoT applications where size and power are critical constraints. The ultra-low 60mW power consumption extends battery life significantly compared to higher-resolution sensors. While the 2MP resolution is modest, it's sufficient for basic imaging needs in wearable devices. The compact package enables integration into very small products. We've used this sensor successfully in smartwatch cameras and compact IoT devices.

faqs.question: What makes SC200 suitable for wearable devices?
faqs.answer: The SC200 is ideal for wearables due to: 1) Ultra-low 60mW power consumption extending battery life; 2) Compact 1/5 inch optical format enabling small camera modules; 3) Simple fixed-focus design reducing component count; 4) Low cost for price-sensitive consumer products; 5) Adequate 2MP resolution for basic imaging needs in wearables.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SC200 for wearables and IoT where power and size are critical. Use SC500+ for devices requiring higher image quality.
